error.signature: upstream timed out (110: Operation timed out) while reading response header from upstream, client: 10.0.0.1, server: example.com
error.regex: (upstream timed out.*reading response|504 Gateway Time-?out|proxy_read_timeout|Operation timed out.*upstream|upstream.*110.*timed out)

verdict.summary: 504 Gateway Timeout. Backend did not send response headers within proxy_read_timeout (default 60s). Long-running request or backend issue.

dead_ends.action: Set proxy_read_timeout to 3600 or higher globally
dead_ends.why_fails: Extremely long timeouts mask backend failures and tie up nginx worker connections for the duration. Set per-location for specific slow endpoints.

dead_ends.action: Add keepalive connections assuming the timeout is a connection issue
dead_ends.why_fails: proxy_read_timeout is about response time, not connection establishment. Keepalive helps with proxy_connect_timeout, not read timeout.

workarounds.action: Increase proxy_read_timeout for specific slow endpoints only
workarounds.how: location /api/reports { proxy_read_timeout 300s; proxy_pass http://backend; }  # only slow endpoints get longer timeout

workarounds.action: Fix the slow backend response (optimize query, add caching)
workarounds.how: Profile backend: slow query log, APM tracing. Add caching for expensive operations. 504 means the backend is too slow.

workarounds.action: Implement request buffering and async processing for long operations
workarounds.how: Return 202 Accepted immediately; process in background; client polls /status endpoint. Avoids tying up nginx and backend workers.
